patch.value: ADR: test organization convention — module-mirrored spec files, no lane/wave naming

Context: the literal-all-src coverage push (pm-w13j) was executed in temporary parallel 'lanes' (A/B/C) with 'waveN' spec files as a work-tracking device. Decision: that vocabulary must NOT persist in the committed suite. Test files mirror the module under test at tests/unit/<area>/<module>.spec.ts (areas: commands, core/<sub>, scripts/<sub>, packages, plugins, docs-examples), one primary module per file, with conventional describe() titles naming the module/behavior. Consequences: contributors locate a module's tests by path; the 120-files-per-directory static gate is respected via area subdirectories; shared dynamic-import harness lives in tests/helpers/.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Test resolution helpers against a directly-built RuntimeStatusRegistry (resolveRuntimeStatusRegistry(customSchema)), NOT a writeSettings round-trip: writeSettings re-seeds the built-in default statuses (open/in_progress/blocked/closed/...) into schema.statuses on persist, so any 'custom workflow that omits status X' scenario is impossible to construct through settings I/O — the built-in always comes back. Direct registry construction does not re-seed and is the correct, deterministic way to cover registry-fallback branches.

patch.value:
## ADR: Test organization convention — module-mirrored spec files, no lane/wave naming

### Context
During the coverage push, work was tracked in transient 'lane A/B/C' and 'wave' groupings, and spec files accumulated flat in tests/unit/ (peaking at 126 files in one directory). This (a) violated the static-quality-gate directory-density cap (--max-files-per-dir 120) and (b) carried throwaway process vocabulary (lane/wave) into permanent file/dir names, obscuring what each spec actually tests.

### Decision
Spec files MIRROR the source module tree under tests/unit/, and no spec/dir name encodes process artifacts (no lane-x, waveXY, group-A, etc.). Layout:
- src/cli/commands/<cmd>.ts          -> tests/unit/commands/<cmd>-command.spec.ts
- src/cli/<infra>.ts                 -> tests/unit/cli/<infra>.spec.ts
- src/core/<sub>/<mod>.ts            -> tests/unit/core/<sub>/<mod>.spec.ts (e.g. core/search/, core/item/, core/store/, core/telemetry/, core/history/)
- src/mcp/**, src/sdk/**             -> tests/unit/mcp/, tests/unit/sdk/
- src/core/extensions + cli ext cmds -> tests/unit/extensions/
- packages/**                        -> tests/unit/packages/
- scripts/**                         -> tests/unit/scripts/ (release scripts under scripts/release/)
- docs/examples/**, plugins/**       -> tests/unit/docs-examples/, tests/unit/plugins/
Integration tests stay in tests/integration/. Each directory must remain under the 120-file static-gate cap; split into src-mirrored subdirectories before approaching it.

### Consequences
- Spec location is derivable from the source file under test (and vice versa), aiding discovery and review.
- Directory density stays under the static-quality-gate cap structurally, not by ad-hoc moves.
- Relative import depth is a function of nesting (e.g. tests/unit/core/item/ -> ../../../../src/...); enforced by the all-import-resolution check.

### Status
Accepted and implemented (full nesting completed; static quality gate green). Related: pm-m449 (reorganization feature), pm-vks9 (120-cap pressure, resolved), epic pm-xau3, ADR pm-w13j.
Accepted & implemented: all specs nested into src-mirrored subdirs; no lane/wave naming; static gate green.
